includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database

contaminant.name: Wood Rot / Fungal Biodegradation
contaminant.description: Wood rot forms through fungal biodegradation of wood fibers, so discoloration appears and softening occurs with structural weakening over time. This contamination penetrates deeply into material unlike surface dust or chemical stains, and behaves faster on softwoods like pine while slower on hardwoods like oak. Removal challenges arise because rot makes wood brittle, yet laser cleaning succeeds by selectively ablating decayed layers without damaging healthy substrate.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Formaldehyde - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
